tex:
(a) Draw the image of shape A after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 0 \\ -6 \end{pmatrix}$.

(b) Draw the image of shape A after a reflection in the line y = -1.

(c) Describe fully the single transformation that maps shape A onto shape B.

(d) Describe fully the single transformation that maps shape A onto shape C.

(a) A plane has 14 First Class seats, 70 Premium seats and 168 Economy seats.

Find the ratio First Class seats : Premium seats : Economy seats.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

 :  : 

(b) For a morning flight, the costs of tickets are in the ratio

First Class : Premium : Economy = 14 : 6 : 5.

The cost of a Premium ticket is \$114.

Calculate the cost of a First Class ticket and the cost of an Economy ticket.

First Class \$ 
Economy \$ 

(c) For an afternoon flight, the cost of a Premium ticket is reduced from \$114 to \$96.90.

Calculate the percentage reduction in the cost of a ticket.

 \%

(d) When the local time in Athens is 09 00, the local time in Berlin is 08 00.

A plane leaves Athens at 13 15.

It arrives in Berlin at 15 05 local time.

Find the flight time from Athens to Berlin.

 h  min

(e) The distance the plane flies from Athens to Berlin is 1802 km.

Calculate the average speed of the plane.

Give your answer in kilometres per hour.

 km/h

The box-and-whisker plots show the times spent exercising in one week by a group of women and a group of men.

Below are two statements comparing these times.

For each one, write down whether you agree or disagree, giving a reason for your answer.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline
Statement & Agree or disagree & Reason \\
\hline
On average, the women spent less time exercising than the men. & & \\
\hline
The times for the women show less variation than the times for the men. & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) The frequency table shows the times, t minutes, each of 100 children spent exercising in one week.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Time (t minutes) & Frequency \\
\hline
$0 \leq t < 60$ & 41 \\
\hline
$60 \leq t < 100$ & 24 \\
\hline
$100 \leq t < 160$ & 23 \\
\hline
$160 \leq t < 220$ & 8 \\
\hline
$220 \leq t < 320$ & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Calculate an estimate of the mean time.

 min

(b) The information in the frequency table is shown in this cumulative frequency diagram.

Use the cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate of

the 60th percentile,

 min

(c) the number of children who spent more than 3 hours exercising.

(d) A histogram is drawn to show the information in the frequency table.

The height of the bar for the interval $60 \leq t < 100$ is 10.8 cm.

Calculate the height of the bar for the interval $160 \leq t < 220$.

 cm

The diagram shows a field, ABCD, on horizontal ground.

BC = 192 m, CD = 287.9 m, BD = 168 m and AD = 205.8 m.

(a) Calculate angle CBD and show that it rounds to 106.0°, correct to 1 decimal place.

[4]

(b) The bearing of D from B is 038°.

Find the bearing of C from B.

 $^\circ$

Calculate the bearing of D from A.

 $^\circ$

 m$^2$

These are the first four diagrams of a sequence.

The diagrams are made from white dots and black dots.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Diagram & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\
\hline
Number of white dots & 1 & 4 & 9 & 16 \\
\hline
Number of black dots & 0 & 1 & 3 & 6 \\
\hline
Total number of dots & 1 & 5 & 12 & 22 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table for Diagram 5 and Diagram 6.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Diagram & 5 & 6 \\
\hline
Number of white dots & & \\
\hline
Number of black dots & & \\
\hline
Total number of dots & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

[2]

(b) Write an expression, in terms of n, for the number of white dots in Diagram n.

(c) The expression for the total number of dots in Diagram n is

$\dfrac{1}{2}(3n^2 - n)$.

Find the total number of dots in Diagram 8.

(d) Find an expression for the number of black dots in Diagram n.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

(e) T is the total number of dots used to make all of the first n diagrams.

T = an$^3$ + bn$^2$

Find the value of a and the value of b.

You must show all your working.

a = 
b =

(a) Factorise completely.

$3a^2b - ab^2$

(b) Solve the inequality.

$3x + 12 \leq 5x - 3$

(c) Simplify.

$\sqrt[3]{x^2 y^4}$

(d) Solve.

$\dfrac{2}{6} = \dfrac{x}{2 - x}$

x = 

(e) Expand and simplify.

$(x - 2)(x + 5)(2x - 1)$

(f) Alan invests \$200 at a rate of r\% per year compound interest.

After 2 years the value of his investment is \$206.46.

Show that $r^2 + 200r - 323 = 0$.

[3]

(g) Solve the equation $r^2 + 200r - 323 = 0$ to find the rate of interest.

Show all your working and give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.

r =

There are 32 students in a class.

5 do not study any languages.

15 study German (G).

18 study Spanish (S).

(a) Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.

[2]

(b) A student is chosen at random.

Find the probability that the student studies Spanish but not German.

(c) A student who studies German is chosen at random.

Find the probability that this student also studies Spanish.

(d) A bag contains 54 red marbles and some blue marbles.

36\% of the marbles in the bag are red.

Find the number of blue marbles in the bag.

(e) Another bag contains 15 red beads and 10 yellow beads.

Ariana picks a bead at random, records its colour and replaces it in the bag.

She then picks another bead at random.

Find the probability that she picks two red beads.

(f) Find the probability that she does not pick two red beads.

(g) A box contains 15 red pencils, 8 yellow pencils and 2 green pencils.

Two pencils are picked at random without replacement.

Find the probability that at least one pencil is red.
